name: Amazon SES API
what_it_does: Amazon Simple Email Service — a highly scalable, low-cost email sending API for transactional, marketing, and bulk email with SMTP and REST interfaces.
use_cases: Sending transactional emails (receipts, notifications, alerts) at low cost
High-volume bulk email campaigns for marketing
Building custom email infrastructure with full control over sending
Receiving and processing inbound email for automated workflows
Monitoring email deliverability with bounce/complaint tracking
not_for: Non-AWS workloads where setup complexity isn't justified
Teams needing email marketing UIs or drag-and-drop templates
Simple transactional email without AWS infrastructure (use Resend or Postmark)
best_when: You're on AWS, need the lowest possible per-email cost for high volume sending, and have engineering resources to manage deliverability.
avoid_when: You need a managed email platform with marketing features, deliverability tooling, or a simpler developer experience.

auth.notes: AWS SigV4 for API calls. SMTP credentials (derived from IAM) for SMTP interface. IAM policies for fine-grained access control. Least-privilege IAM recommended.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 62k emails/month free when sending from EC2; otherwise 3k free/month
pricing.notes: Extremely low cost — $100 sends 1 million emails. Sandbox mode (default) restricts to verified addresses. Request production access to send to unverified addresses.

performance.rate_limits: 14 emails/second default; can be increased by request

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: AWS-standard XML/JSON error responses with error codes and descriptive messages. Well-documented error catalog.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Email sending is not idempotent — same call sends duplicate emails. Use application-level deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: New accounts start in sandbox mode — can only send to verified email addresses until production access approved
Production access requires manual approval from AWS — can take hours/days
Must verify sending domain and From address before sending
Bounce and complaint rates must be monitored — high rates get account suspended
Configuration sets required for proper tracking and event notifications
SES v2 API (newer) and SES v1 (legacy) exist with different SDKs — use v2 for new projects

security_score_components.security_notes: IAM policies with ses:SendEmail, ses:SendRawEmail permissions. Domain/email verification required. DKIM signing enforced on verified domains. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP. Sandbox mode by default — request production access for real sending.
